top_findings.finding: Participant expressed inability to compare plans side by side and stated this limitation prevented meaningful assessment of the comparison table.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant stated desire to see information side by side, then explicitly said 'by not seeing it side by side, I cannot really, um, comment on the table itself' and 'it's not a table really, is it?'
top_findings.stated_signal: I would like to see it side to side to see what's covered, what's not covered, so I could see what's included, excluded, what's better, what's not, um, by not seeing it side by side, I cannot really, um, comment on the table itself, because it's not a table really, is it?
top_findings.rationale: The participant expected a side-by-side comparison format (typical table pattern) but the current layout does not support this. The stated signal directly indicates the interface did not behave as expected for comparison tasks, and the participant described this as preventing full assessment. This is significant component friction that was worked around (participant proceeded with preliminary decision only) rather than blocked completely.

top_findings.finding: Participant could not locate the table on the page and expressed frustration with the layout's horizontal scrolling constraint.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant searched for the table, stated inability to find it, and expressed dissatisfaction with the page layout preventing side-to-side viewing.
top_findings.stated_signal: But where is the table? I can't even find the table. I'm struggling to find the table. Um, I mean, it's got information on it. But it is not allowing me to see it from side to side.
top_findings.rationale: The participant explicitly stated inability to locate the table and repeated the struggle twice. The stated signal aligns with the observed inability to find required content. Combined with negative sentiment about the layout constraint, this represents significant friction blocking access to needed information.

top_findings.finding: Participant experienced accessibility friction due to horizontal layout constraints preventing full content visibility.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant expressed inability to see content from side to side and indicated the layout design is preventing proper content access.
top_findings.stated_signal: it's not allowing me to see it from side to side.
top_findings.rationale: The participant stated that the horizontal scrolling requirement prevents content visibility. This is an accessibility barrier that restricts the participant's ability to view information in an accessible manner, matching the F3 definition and representing significant friction to content consumption.

top_findings.finding: Participant attempted to navigate to a health assessment page but the link did not work, requiring multiple recovery attempts and backtracking.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant tried to click a link, stated it didn't work, then attempted to go back multiple times before deciding to try typing or finding an alternative pathway.
top_findings.stated_signal: It didn't work. It didn't go to that page, so I'm trying to se go back. And let's see and go back to it. No, let's go back to it.
top_findings.rationale: The participant's observed behaviour (multiple back attempts, recovery strategy shift) and explicit statement that the link 'didn't work' indicate the interface failed to respond as expected. The participant recovered by exploring alternatives, but the repeated backtracking and frustration signal significant friction matching the L3 gate (struggle, multiple attempts, task eventually completed).

top_findings.finding: Participant expressed confusion and uncertainty about the complexity and simplicity of the bottom section of the page, stating it was 'not simple' despite claims that it is.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant paused, hesitated, and described struggling to understand whether the bottom section was actually simple or too technical.
top_findings.stated_signal: I just struggle with uh tech stuff and there's the bottom part where it is. Um, This part, it's not simple, really. They say it is, but I don't think it is.
top_findings.rationale: The participant stated explicit confusion about the clarity and simplicity of the bottom section, describing it as contradictory to its label and too technical. The hesitation and restatement indicate comprehension friction, but the participant did not abandon the task and continued engagement, placing this at medium friction rather than severe.
top_findings.structural_amplification_note: Participant self-identified as struggling with tech content, which amplified the friction experienced when encountering language or concepts perceived as overly technical.

coaching_recommendations.title: Review high-friction moments before lower-level issues
coaching_recommendations.summary: The session contains 18 finding-level issue(s), with the highest severity at S3.
advice: Identify the common pattern behind the higher-severity findings.
Focus coaching on the most disruptive parts of the session first.
Avoid treating all findings as equal when severity levels differ.
coaching_recommendations.evidence_note: 5.1-A severity distribution: S3=1, S4=3, S5=14. Representative findings: Participant expressed inability to compare plans side by side and stated this limitation prevented meaningful assessment of the comparison table. | Participant could not locate the table on the page and expressed frustration with the layout's horizontal scrolling constraint. | Participant experienced accessibility friction due to horizontal layout constraints preventing full content visibility.

coaching_recommendations.title: Multi-pattern friction across 6 categories — anchor coaching on the dominant pattern
coaching_recommendations.summary: This session shows 18 findings spanning 6 distinct friction types (F6,F1,F4,F2,F3,F5). Coaching should follow the dominant pattern first rather than issuing one item per type.
advice: After addressing any severity-priority recommendation first, use this aggregation to group the remaining coaching themes — do not treat the two as parallel.
Treat F6 (Not Found) as the primary coaching anchor; secondary types (F1 Comprehension, F4 Unresponsive) provide supporting context.
Avoid issuing parallel recommendations for each friction type when patterns interact within the same session.
coaching_recommendations.evidence_note: 5.1-A: total=18 findings across 6 distinct friction types (F6=6, F1=4, F4=3, F2=2, F3=2, F5=1).

coaching_recommendations.title: Recording quality is usable but should be improved
coaching_recommendations.summary: The session is still usable for analysis, but defects in the recording may reduce confidence in detailed interpretation.
advice: Check microphone clarity and ambient noise before starting future sessions.
Reduce clipping, brief audio gaps, or interruptions where possible.
Mark this session as usable with caution if fine-grained analysis is required.
coaching_recommendations.evidence_note: 5.1-B marks recording quality as acceptable rather than good.
